title: Improper File Upload Validation
description: Learn how weak file upload validation allows attackers to upload malicious files that can compromise a web application.

attack_stages.title: File Upload
attack_stages.description: The website allows users to upload files such as images, documents, or profile pictures.

attack_stages.title: Weak File Validation
attack_stages.description: The website does not properly verify uploaded file types, content, or size.

attack_stages.title: Malicious File Upload
attack_stages.description: An attacker uploads a harmful file disguised as a legitimate document or image.

attack_stages.title: Malicious File Processing
attack_stages.description: The uploaded file is stored or processed by the server, allowing the attacker to abuse the application.

attack_stages.title: System Compromise
attack_stages.description: The attacker gains unauthorized access, executes malicious actions, or compromises sensitive data.

prevention_points.title: Allow Approved File Types
prevention_points.description: Accept only specific file types that are required by the application.

prevention_points.title: Verify File Content
prevention_points.description: Inspect the actual file contents instead of trusting file extensions.

prevention_points.title: Malware Scanning
prevention_points.description: Scan uploaded files before storing or processing them.

prevention_points.title: Secure File Storage
prevention_points.description: Store uploaded files outside publicly accessible directories.

nodes.title: Scenario
nodes.text: A company website allows users to upload files such as resumes, profile pictures, and documents.

nodes.title: How File Uploads Work
nodes.text: When a user uploads a file, the website receives it, validates it, stores it, and may later display or process it.

nodes.title: Improper File Upload Validation
nodes.text: The website only checks the file extension and does not verify the actual file type or content. As a result, malicious files can bypass validation and be uploaded successfully.

nodes.title: Attacker Goal
nodes.text: The attacker wants to upload a malicious file that the website accepts as a legitimate upload.

nodes.title: What allows the attacker to upload a malicious file?

choices.text: The website only checks the file extension instead of the actual file content.
The website automatically deletes every uploaded file.

nodes.title: Try Again
nodes.text: Weak upload validation occurs when websites trust file names or extensions instead of verifying what the file actually contains.

nodes.title: Malicious File Accepted
nodes.text: The website stores and processes the uploaded file because it passed the weak validation checks. The attacker can now exploit how the application handles that file.

nodes.title: Impact
nodes.text: Improper file upload validation can allow attackers to execute malicious code, spread malware, overwrite files, or gain unauthorized access to the application.

nodes.title: How to Prevent This
nodes.text: Organizations should allow only approved file types, verify the actual file contents, scan uploaded files for malware, enforce file size limits, and store uploaded files outside publicly accessible directories.

nodes.title: Attack Replay
nodes.text: Review how weak upload validation allowed a malicious file to bypass security checks and compromise the web application.

nodes.title: Scenario Complete
nodes.text: Improper file upload validation can expose a web application to malware and other attacks. Validating file content, scanning uploads, and securely storing files are essential security practices.
